original_hypothesis.title: burnAddress Misconfiguration Can Permanently DoS lockAndBurn, Blocking Round Progression
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The initialize() function does not validate burnAddress. If burnAddress is set to address(0) and the GTC token reverts on zero-address transfers (as the production GTC does), lockAndBurn will permanently revert whenever amountToBurn > 0, blocking all future round progression and preventing any further slashed funds from being burned.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The initialize function validates tokenAddress != address(0) but does NOT validate burnAddress. The deployment script (deploy.ts) deploys a 'Lock' contract, not IdentityStaking, providing no evidence of burnAddress validation. GTC's _transferTokens requires dst != address(0).
deployment_assumptions: burnAddress is set to a valid non-zero address that can receive GTC transfers
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: IdentityStaking.sol initialize(): no validation on _burnAddress parameter; deploy.ts does not deploy IdentityStaking, so no deployment evidence of burnAddress value

proof_of_signal.lines: initialize() - burnAddress = _burnAddress with no validation; lockAndBurn() - token.transfer(burnAddress, amountToBurn)
proof_of_signal.pattern: No address(0) check on burnAddress; if set to 0, token.transfer to zero address reverts in most ERC20s

preconditions: burnAddress set to address(0) or an address that cannot receive tokens
Any non-zero slash exists in a round

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: GTC._transferTokens requires dst != address(0); lockAndBurn calls token.transfer(burnAddress, amount)

analysis_questions: What burnAddress was used in actual deployment?
Can burnAddress be changed post-deployment? (No setter found)
Does the GTC token on mainnet/Optimism revert on zero-address transfers?
validation_requirements: Verify no setter for burnAddress exists (requires upgrade to change)
Test lockAndBurn with burnAddress=address(0) and amountToBurn > 0

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If burnAddress is misconfigured at deployment, all lockAndBurn calls revert when slashed amount > 0. Round progression halts, slashed amounts cannot be burned, and new slashing rounds cannot begin. Only a contract upgrade can fix this.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Slashing mechanism completely disabled; slashed tokens cannot be burned
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Contract upgrade required to fix burnAddress

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable immediately on first lockAndBurn attempt

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
